records.ety: [OE. knel, cnul, AS. cnyll, fr. cnyllan to sound a bell; cf. D. & G. knallen to clap, crack, G. & Sw. knall a clap, crack, loud sound, Dan. knalde to clap, crack. Cf. Knoll, n. & v.]

defs: The stroke of a bell tolled at a funeral or at the death of a person; a death signal; a passing bell;
a warning or harbinger of, or a sound indicating, the passing away of anything; -- also called death knell.
quotes: The dead man's knell Is there scarce asked for who.
The curfew tolls the knell of parting day.

records.ety: [OE. knellen, knillen, As. cnyllan. See Knell, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Knelled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Knelling.]

defs: To sound as a knell; especially, to toll at a death or funeral; hence, to sound as a warning or evil omen.
quotes: Not worth a blessing nor a bell to knell for thee.
Yet all that poets sing, and grief hath known, Of hopes laid waste, knells in that word, “alone”.

defs: To summon, as by a knell.
quotes: Each matin bell, the baron saith, Knells us back to a world of death.
